sections.label: Section 3.1.45
sections.translation: Why with rich gate and pillard range

sections.label: Section 3.1.46
sections.translation: Upbuild new mansions , twice as high ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.47
sections.translation: Or why my Sabine vale exchange

sections.label: Section 3.1.48
sections.translation: For more laborious luxury ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.1
sections.translation: Yet again thou wak ' st the flame

sections.label: Section 4.1.2
sections.original: rursus bella moves ? parce , precor precor .
sections.translation: That long had slumber ' d ! Spare me , Venus , spare !

sections.label: Section 4.1.3
sections.translation: Trust me , I am not the same

sections.label: Section 4.1.4
sections.translation: As in the reign of Cinara , kind and fair .

sections.label: Section 4.1.5
sections.translation: Cease thy softening spells to prove

sections.label: Section 4.1.6
sections.translation: On this old heart , by fifty years made hard ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.7
sections.translation: Cruel Mother of sweet Love !

sections.label: Section 4.1.8
sections.original: quo blandae iuvenum te revocant preces .
sections.translation: Haste , where gay youth solicits thy regard .

sections.label: Section 4.1.9
sections.translation: With thy purple cygnets fly

sections.label: Section 4.1.10
sections.translation: To Paullus ' door , a seasonable guest ;

sections.label: Section 4.1.11
sections.translation: There within hold revelry ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.12
sections.translation: There light thy flame in that congenial breast .

sections.label: Section 4.1.13
sections.translation: He , with birth and beauty graced ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.14
sections.original: et pro sollicitis non tacitus reis
sections.translation: The trembling client ' s champion , ne ' er tongue-tied ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.15
sections.translation: Master of each manly taste ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.16
sections.translation: Shall bear thy conquering banners far and wide .

sections.label: Section 4.1.17
sections.translation: Let him smile in triumph gay ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.18
sections.translation: True heart , victorious over lavish hand ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.19
sections.translation: By the Alban lake that day

sections.label: Section 4.1.20
sections.translation: ' Neath citron roof all marble shalt thou stand :

sections.label: Section 4.1.21
sections.translation: Incense there and fragrant spice

sections.label: Section 4.1.22
sections.translation: With odorous fumes thy nostrils shall salute ;

sections.label: Section 4.1.23
sections.translation: Blended notes thine ear entice ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.24
sections.translation: The lyre , the pipe , the Berecyntine flute :

sections.label: Section 4.1.25
sections.translation: Graceful youths and maidens bright

sections.label: Section 4.1.26
sections.translation: Shall twice a day thy tuneful praise resound ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.27
sections.translation: While their feet , so fair and white ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.28
sections.original: in morem Salium ter quatient humum .
sections.translation: In Salian measure three times beat the ground .

sections.label: Section 4.1.29
sections.translation: I can relish love no more ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.30
sections.original: iam nec spes animi credula mutui
sections.translation: Nor flattering hopes that tell me hearts are true ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.31
sections.translation: Nor the revel ' s loud uproar ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.32
sections.translation: Nor fresh-wreathed flowerets , bathed in vernal dew .

sections.label: Section 4.1.33
sections.translation: Ah ! but why , my Ligurine ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.34
sections.original: manat rara meas lacrima per genas ?
sections.translation: Steal trickling tear-drops down my wasted cheek ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.35
sections.translation: Wherefore halts this tongue of mine ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.36
sections.translation: So eloquent once , so faltering now and weak ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.37
sections.translation: Now I hold you in my chain ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.38
sections.original: iam captum teneo , iam volucrem sequor
sections.translation: And clasp you close , all in a nightly dream ;

sections.label: Section 4.1.39
sections.translation: Now , still dreaming , o ' er the plain

sections.label: Section 4.1.40
sections.original: campi , te per aquas , dure , volubilis .
sections.translation: I chase you ; now , ah cruel ! down the stream .
